zh.lifeSummary: Christian Pile於1530年出生在英國戴文希德波利。她於1551年與Richard Clapp在英國英格蘭戴文希德波利結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1609年7月25日在她的家鄉享年79歲，埋葬在英國戴文希德波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Christian Pile于1530年出生在英国戴文希德波利。她于1551年与Richard Clapp在英国英格兰戴文希德波利结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1609年7月25日在她的家乡享年79岁，埋葬在英国戴文希德波利。

nameMeanings.name: Pile
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon and London): variant of   Pyle  .

French: from Old French pile ‘mortar, trough’ (from Latin pila), denoting a trough used for crushing or pounding various materials, such as lime, and in some cases the surname may have arisen as a metonymic occupational name for someone engaged in such work.
French: topographic name from Old French pile ‘pillar, column’ (from Latin pila), or a habitational name from any of the minor places in northern France named with this word.

variants.name: Pilot
meanings: English: from Middle English Pilat, Pilet, Pilot (from Latin Pīlatus), used either as personal name or as nickname for a boastful person or for one who played the part of Pilate in the medieval mystery plays, where he was portrayed as a proud, ranting tyrant.

French: diminutive of Pile ‘mortar, trough’ or a topographic name (referring e.g. to a culminating point) or nickname (for a tall, thin person) based on Old French pilot ‘post, stake’. It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin.
Italian (Friuli): from old Friulian pilot ‘spike, bodkin’. Compare Pillot .

variants.name: Pyle
meanings: English: from Middle English pile ‘stake, post’ (via Old English from Latin pilum ‘spike, javelin’), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near a stake or post serving as a landmark, or sometimes perhaps a nickname for a tall thin person.
Americanized form of Dutch Pijl: metonymic occupational name for a marksman or an arrowsmith, from pijl ‘arrow’.

variants.name: Pilon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Henri, Jean-Paul, Marcel, Adelore, Aime, Alain, Anatole, Armand, Aurore, Elzear, Emile.
French: habitational name from (Le) Pilon, the name of several places in various parts of France, ultimately derived from Latin pila ‘pillar, column’. Compare Pelo , Pelon , and Pillon .

nameMeanings.name: Christian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

variants.name: Christianne
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Christian Pile was born in 1530, in Sidbury, Devon, England. She married Richard Clapp in 1551, in Sidbury, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 25 July 1609, in her hometown, at the age of 79, and was buried in Sidbury, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Christian Pile est née en 1530, à Sidbury, Devon, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Richard Clapp en 1551, à Sidbury, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 juillet 1609, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à Sidbury, Devon, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Christian Pileは1530年にイギリスデボンシドブリーで生まれた。1551年にイギリスイギリスデボンシドブリーでRichard Clappと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1609年7月25日に故郷で79歳で死去し，イギリスデボンシドブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Christian Pile(은)는 1530년 영국 데폰 시드버이에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1551년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 시드버이에서 Richard Clapp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1609년 7월 25일 그녀의 고향에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 시드버이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Christian Pile родилась в 1530 г. в местности сидбури, девон, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Clapp в 1551 г. в местности сидбури, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 25 июля 1609 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности сидбури, девон, Англия.
